name: Stephen King
description: The King of Horror — Carrie, It, The Shining, Misery, and the Dark Tower.

questions.q: What pen name did Stephen King famously publish under?

source.notes: Pseudonym used to publish more than once a year. Outed in 1985.

questions.q: What's Stephen King's middle name?

source.notes: Stephen Edwin King.

questions.q: What's the name of Stephen King's wife (also a successful novelist)?

source.notes: Married 1971.

questions.q: Which US state are most of Stephen King's stories set in?

source.notes: Recurring fictional towns: Derry, Castle Rock, Jerusalem's Lot.

questions.q: What's the name of the haunted clown in 'It'?

source.notes: Pennywise the Dancing Clown.

questions.q: What car is the haunted vehicle in Stephen King's 'Christine'?

source.notes: Always plays 50s oldies on the radio.

questions.q: What breed of dog is the title character in 'Cujo'?

source.notes: Goes rabid after a bat bite.

questions.q: In 'Misery', what implement does Annie Wilkes use to hobble Paul Sheldon (in the novel)?
choices: Both — book is sledgehammer, film is sledgehammer (axe is a misconception)

source.notes: The book has Annie use an axe to chop off his foot, then a blowtorch to cauterize. The film softened it to sledgehammer-to-the-ankle.

questions.q: What's the name of the cat that's resurrected in 'Pet Sematary'?

source.notes: Short for 'Winston Churchill.'

questions.q: In 'The Green Mile', what does John Coffey want as his last wish?

source.notes: He'd never seen one. They show him 'Top Hat.'

questions.q: What's the name of the mouse in 'The Green Mile'?

source.notes: Belongs to inmate Delacroix.

questions.q: What's Stephen King's epic 8-book sci-fi/western/horror series called?

source.notes: 8 main novels (1982-2012) + connected stories.

questions.q: Who is the main character of The Dark Tower series?

source.notes: Last Gunslinger. Played by Idris Elba in the 2017 film.

questions.q: What 1986 epic novel features the kids of the 'Losers' Club' in Derry, Maine?

source.notes: 1,138 pages.

questions.q: What's Stephen King's 1974 debut novel about a telekinetic teenager?

source.notes: Tabitha rescued the manuscript from the trash.

questions.q: What's the haunted hotel in 'The Shining'?

source.notes: Inspired by King's stay at the real Stanley Hotel in Estes Park, CO.

questions.q: What apocalyptic Stephen King epic has a superflu wipe out 99.4% of humanity?

source.notes: Originally published 1978; uncut edition 1990.

questions.q: Which baseball team does Stephen King famously support?

source.notes: Co-wrote 'Faithful' (2004) about the Red Sox season.

questions.q: What university did Stephen King attend?

source.notes: Graduated 1970 with a B.A. in English.

questions.q: What 1982 novella collection contains 'Rita Hayworth and Shawshank Redemption'?

source.notes: Also contains 'The Body' (Stand By Me) and 'Apt Pupil.'
